quality.bugNote: 12 bugs attributable to incorrect or incomplete test specifications — the tests themselves encoded wrong assumptions about D&D 5e rules or codebase interaction patterns, leading to implementations that passed tests but exhibited incorrect behavior

notes: Human developer following TDD patterns. The 68 compile/run errors represent the iterative development cycle across 6 sub-features spanning domain rules, application services, infrastructure adapters, and E2E scenarios. The 12 bugs were caused by incorrect test assertions — the developer wrote tests that encoded wrong expectations (e.g., wrong timing for rage-end checks, incorrect damage resistance scope, misunderstood interaction between dual-path turn management), leading to code that passed tests but did not correctly implement D&D 5e 2024 rules. These required substantial rework once discovered through manual play testing or cross-referencing rulebook text.
